body:
After the sepo.js swap the drawer chrome used the embed's neutral fallback palette instead of the site colors (visible as a mismatched drawer background and a differently-colored "click me" hint). The --sepo-* custom properties on the .sepo-comments host are the embed's designed theming channel (they pierce the shadow DOM), so this maps the Quartz palette onto them — dark mode included, since Quartz flips the underlying variables.


🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | PR #30 changes only `quartz/styles/custom.scss` with 13 added lines, all scoped to `.sepo-comments`: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/43149b2fab635f57409305052bf8c81f67c07fc0/quartz/styles/custom.scss#L1248-L1259 |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The change uses the existing service-owned `sepo.js` host contract instead of reintroducing local drawer styling; the component still only emits config for the service drawer: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/43149b2fab635f57409305052bf8c81f67c07fc0/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L44-L46 |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | No new config or platform abstraction is added; the template maps its existing Quartz CSS variables onto the embed’s native `--sepo-*` theming channel: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/43149b2fab635f57409305052bf8c81f67c07fc0/quartz/styles/custom.scss#L1252-L1258 |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation is a single declarative CSS custom-property block, with no runtime code, workflow changes, or duplicated drawer implementation. |

## Notes

- The selected process, authorization, preview, command-routing, setup-docs, and status-comment rubrics were not scored because this PR is a CSS-only palette mapping and does not change those surfaces.
- I did not score `Keep docs in sync`: PR #30 does not add a public setup option or change the documented comments configuration; the existing README still describes `sepo.js` as the drawer runtime and the site theme as the driver for the widget: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/43149b2fab635f57409305052bf8c81f67c07fc0/README.md#L162-L166
- GitHub reports the PR `check` job and Vercel deployment passing. A local `npm run check` could not complete because this checkout is missing installed dependencies.
- `self-evolving/comment-api#13` was not readable with the available token, so this review relies on the PR body plus the local template-side contract.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

There was no trusted, reusable implementation/review/communication preference to learn beyond existing rubrics like surgical changes, reuse existing code, and minimal native escape hatches.

Rubrics checkout remains unchanged.

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 43149b2fab635f57409305052bf8c81f67c07fc0 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #30 maps the site’s Quartz palette onto the Sepo drawer host’s `--sepo-*` CSS custom-property contract. The goal is to fix the drawer chrome using the embed fallback palette after the `sepo.js` swap. The change is CSS-only and scoped to `quartz/styles/custom.scss`. The PR is already merged.

## Review
All available reviewer outputs agree this is safe to ship. There are no blocking or warning findings, and the only notes are optional future sync/documentation considerations.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues | INFO | Reviewers found the implementation scoped, correct, and aligned with the embed theming contract. No required branch-change work remains. |

## Progress
- Current PR conversation, diff, inline comments, and review threads were inspected.
- No inline comments were posted because there were no concrete file/line-specific issues.
- No existing inline comments or review threads needed cleanup.
- Optional notes about future embed contract drift and README discoverability were treated as non-blocking.

## Recommended Next Step
`NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: no unresolved actionable work remains, and the PR is already merged.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change work remains.

title: Map the site palette onto the Sepo drawer's theming contract
